mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2013 payroll growth of 150k+ jobs signals post-recession recovery acceleration
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held near zero combined with declining inflation expectations signals monetary accommodation that reduces real discount rates on equities, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as investors rotate into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.14% with CPI at 230.979
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary easing with subdued inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window during zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in retail sales growth above 0.5% monthly rate signals strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:January 2013 retail sales at 416,633 million dollars
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2013 INDPRO at 98.6212 showing post-recession volatility
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above 900,000 annualized units combined with retail sales growth signals broad-based demand recovery that increases corporate capital expenditure expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2012 HOUST at 954k units, January 2013 RSAFS at 416,633 million
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declining below 8.0% signals labor market tightening that increases wage pressure and consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate higher corporate revenues from increased consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2013 UNRATE at 7.9% showing post-recession improvement
regime_hint: labor market tightening in recovery phase
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration above 2.5% annualized combined with rising payroll employment signals broad-based economic expansion that increases corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 140k
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2012 GDP recovery with January 2013 payroll gains
regime_hint: synchronized demand and labor market expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 38% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.065 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 0.5% signals negative real rates that erode bond returns and force investors into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as portfolio reallocation accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2013 CPI at 230.979 with FEDFUNDS at 0.14%
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.045 over any 2-year rolling window.
